name: Mountain biking through the forges of Artikutza
name_original: Una vuelta en BTT por las ferrerías de Artikutza
description:
In the Sierra de Artikutza, several forges active until 1919 left old buildings and remains of the train that transported minerals, silent witnesses of its industrial past. This Route takes you into the heart of the Guipuzcoan forest to discover these vestiges and the wildest nature of the area.

You can arrive at the start of the tour in your own vehicle and park it in the designated area next to the Artikutza quarry. Once you start pedaling, you will immediately feel the magic of the Navarrese forest. The itinerary, made up of trails and tracks with challenging sections, will test your skills on the bike.

In the village of Artikutza there are vending machines for food and water. However, we recommend that you bring enough liquid and snacks to enjoy a picnic while you contemplate the wonderful views of this special corner. The weather is usually cool and humid, so it is advisable to wear waterproof clothing and be prepared for possible rain.
region_intro: Hiking around Goizueta offers access to diverse landscapes within Navarra, Spain. The region is characterized by extensive natural forests of beech, oak, and yew trees, particularly within the Artikutza Natural Park. It features a mountainous cirque with peaks such as Bianditz and Arainburu, alongside river systems like the Urumea and notable waterfalls. This terrain provides varied elevation changes and natural features for outdoor exploration. Best hiking trails around Goizueta The most popular hiking route is Bianditz Summit – Masuketa (795 m) loop from Eskas, a 4.1 miles (6.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 5 minutes to complete. This route features varied terrain and views of the surrounding peaks. Another top favourite among local hikers is Artikutza Village and Valley – Erroiarri Waterfall loop from Eskas, a difficult 10.0 miles (16.1 km) path. This trail leads through dense forests to a notable waterfall. Local hikers also love the Almost at the end of the high part – View of Bordaberri loop from Goizueta, a 4.0 miles (6.5 km) trail leading through rural landscapes and viewpoints, often completed in about 2 hours 5 minutes. Hiking around Goizueta is defined by dense forests of beech and oak, mountainous cirques, and river valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels. The routes in Goizueta are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 700 reviews. More than 4000 hikers have used komoot to explore Goizueta's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Goizueta?
A: There are over 18 hiking trails around Goizueta, offering a range of options for different skill levels, from moderate to difficult.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or casual walkers?
A: While there are no trails officially classified as 'easy' in the guide data, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20520638" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almost at the end of the high part – View of Bordaberri loop from Goizueta&lt;/a&gt; are described as suitable for all skill levels in the region research, offering accessible paths through rural landscapes.

Q: Where can I find challenging hikes in Goizueta?
A: For a more challenging experience, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3993035" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Artikutza Village and Valley – Erroiarri Waterfall loop from Eskas&lt;/a&gt;. This difficult 16.1 km path involves significant elevation gain and leads through dense forests. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5345769" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bianditz Summit – Masuketa (795 m) loop from Eskas&lt;/a&gt;, which requires good fitness and sure-footedness.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Goizueta?
A: Yes, many of the trails in Goizueta are circular. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5345769" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bianditz Summit – Masuketa (795 m) loop from Eskas&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3993035" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Artikutza Village and Valley – Erroiarri Waterfall loop from Eskas&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20520638" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almost at the end of the high part – View of Bordaberri loop from Goizueta&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I see waterfalls while hiking in Goizueta?
A: Yes, the region is known for its impressive waterfalls. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3993035" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Artikutza Village and Valley – Erroiarri Waterfall loop from Eskas&lt;/a&gt; specifically leads to the Erroiarri Waterfall. You can also find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1841170" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aitzondo Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; nearby.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see on the trails?
A: Goizueta's trails wind through diverse landscapes, including extensive natural forests of beech, oak, and yew trees, especially within the Artikutza Natural Park. You'll also encounter mountainous cirques with peaks like Bianditz and Arainburu, river systems such as the Urumea, and impressive waterfalls.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or points of interest along the hiking routes?
A: Beyond natural beauty, Goizueta's trails can lead to megalithic remains and medieval structures. Notable historical sites include the Ibero Tower, an old ironworks, and the 16th-century stone bridge over the Urumea River. You can also explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3065940" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Juan Xar Cave Shrine&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5113984" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Irugurutzeta Furnaces&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Goizueta?
A: The hiking routes in Goizueta are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 700 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, the tranquility of the dense forests, and the stunning views of the surrounding peaks and natural features.

Q: What is the typical duration for hikes in Goizueta?
A: Hike durations vary significantly based on the route and your pace. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5345769" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bianditz Summit – Masuketa (795 m) loop from Eskas&lt;/a&gt; typically takes about 2 hours and 5 minutes, while the more challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3993035" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Artikutza Village and Valley – Erroiarri Waterfall loop from Eskas&lt;/a&gt; can take around 5 hours and 41 minutes.

Q: Is Goizueta a good destination for family-friendly hikes?
A: Goizueta offers options suitable for various abilities. While specific 'family-friendly' classifications aren't provided, routes described as 'suitable for all skill levels' like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20520638" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almost at the end of the high part – View of Bordaberri loop from Goizueta&lt;/a&gt;, with its accessible paths, could be good choices for families.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in Goizueta?
A: The region's natural parks and trails are generally welcoming to dogs, but it's always recommended to keep them on a leash, especially in protected areas like Artikutza Natural Park, to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike in Goizueta?
A: Goizueta is known as one of the wettest areas in the Basque Country. While hiking is possible year-round, spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and lush scenery. Summer can be warm, and winter hikes are possible but be prepared for potentially wet conditions and cooler weather.
tips: The landscape that surrounds the Artikutza quarry is wonderful at any time of the year that you visit it. At the gates of the Protected Zone, the quarry is a starting point for several paths and paths that always satisfy both hikers and mountain bikers.

This was one of the most prominent forges in the region until the 19th century. Today, you can still recognize the potential it had during its period of operation. The old and new bridges, along with other buildings such as the mill, the workers' house and the dam, attest to this.

This small and picturesque town is undoubtedly worth visiting. It is located within the protected area and in a very quiet and attractive environment. It is an ideal place to take a break and enjoy the landscape. Here, you will find vending machines, bathrooms and public toilets, a fountain, and a recreational area.

Not only is it a beautiful waterfall of almost 20 m, but also the environment that surrounds it is wonderful and very picturesque. The forest is an ideal place to visit and explore in the summer months, the shade and fresh air will be your great reward!
This viewpoint a few meters from the top of Erroiarri offers beautiful views of the entire protected area of Artikutza and the Erroiarri river valley.

waypoints.name: View of the Erroiarri Valley and Artikutza
